name: zoho_invoice
title: Create a GST Invoice on Zoho
description: Create a professional invoice with multiple line items, GST calculation, client details, and download as PDF.
prompt: Go to Zoho Invoice, create a new invoice for 'Acme Corp' with 3 line items including GST, preview and download as PDF
narration_script_text:
Listen up, freelancers and small biz heroes! Tired of sending invoices that look like they were made in MS Paint? Today, I'm showing you how to go from broke to professional in under two minutes using Zoho's free invoice generator. No login, no soul-crushing forms, just pure efficiency. Let's ride!

Look at that clean UI. Fresh breath of mountain air for your finances. Boom, we're in the cockpit. Time to tell the world who you are. Type in your company name, your name, and your address. Deep breath. The internet is doing its magic. We're officially a business entity, people! Who's paying us? Enter Acme Corp International. Give them a professional address in Mumbai. We aren't just sending a bill; we're sending a statement. Give this masterpiece a number. INV-2026-001, the first of many!

Now let's add our first line item: Web Development Services. Forty hours of your genius, don't lowball yourself! Set that GST rate and watch the numbers dance.

Let's stack those services. UI/UX Design? Check. Annual Server Hosting? Check. Now we let Zoho do the heavy lifting. We are not here for mental math. I didn't come here to calculate 9% of 160,000 in my head; I have a life to live!

Step nine: drop in the fine print. Payment due within 30 days. Add the bank details. Quick pause while the totals update. It's like a slot machine spin, except you actually win.

Step ten: review the subtotal and grand total. That's the sound of success.

Everything looks crisp and legal. Hit Download/Print, export the PDF, and send it off. Tutorial complete. Your coffee is still cooling. Go get that bread!

steps.caption: Step 1: Open Zoho Invoice - free online invoicing tool for businesses
steps.description: Open Zoho Invoice - free online invoicing tool for businesses
steps.narration: Listen up, freelancers and small biz heroes! Tired of sending invoices that look like they were made in MS Paint? Today, I'm showing you how to go from broke to professional in under two minutes using Zoho's free invoice generator. No login, no soul-crushing forms, just pure efficiency. Let's ride!

steps.caption: Step 2: Open the free invoice generator page - no login required
steps.description: Open the free invoice generator page - no login required
steps.narration: The best part? It's free and needs zero logins. No passwords to forget today. Fire up the free generator page and let's get this bread.

steps.caption: Step 3: Enter business details: company name, contact, and address
steps.description: Enter business details: company name, contact, and address
steps.narration: Look at that clean UI. Fresh breath of mountain air for your finances. Boom, we're in the cockpit. Time to tell the world who you are. Type in your company name, your name, and your address. Deep breath. The internet is doing its magic. We're officially a business entity, people! Who's paying us? Enter Acme Corp International. Give them a professional address in Mumbai. We aren't just sending a bill; we're sending a statement. Give this masterpiece a number. INV-2026-001, the first of many!

steps.caption: Step 4: Enter Bill To details for Acme Corp International
steps.description: Enter Bill To details for Acme Corp International
steps.narration: Time to bill the big fish. Enter the client details: Acme Corp International. Drop in their address and lock in the billing state, because the taxman is always watching.

steps.caption: Step 5: Set invoice number to INV-2026-001
steps.narration: Set that invoice number to INV-2026-001. The first invoice is always the sweetest.

steps.caption: Step 6: Add line item 1 with quantity, rate, and GST
steps.description: Add line item 1 with quantity, rate, and GST
steps.narration: Now let's add our first line item: Web Development Services. Forty hours of your genius, don't lowball yourself! Set that GST rate and watch the numbers dance.

steps.caption: Step 7: Add line item 2: UI/UX Design services
steps.description: Add line item 2: UI/UX Design services
steps.narration: Let's stack those services. UI/UX Design? Check. Annual Server Hosting? Check. Now we let Zoho do the heavy lifting. We are not here for mental math. I didn't come here to calculate 9% of 160,000 in my head; I have a life to live!

steps.caption: Step 8: Add line item 3: annual server hosting
steps.description: Add line item 3: annual server hosting
steps.narration: One more for the road! Line item three: Annual Server Hosting. Eighteen thousand flat.

steps.caption: Step 9: Add payment notes and terms for the client
steps.description: Add payment notes and terms for the client
steps.narration: Step nine: drop in the fine print. Payment due within 30 days. Add the bank details. Quick pause while the totals update. It's like a slot machine spin, except you actually win.

steps.caption: Step 10: Review subtotal, GST breakdown, and grand total
steps.description: Review subtotal, GST breakdown, and grand total
steps.narration: Step ten: review the subtotal and grand total. That's the sound of success.

steps.caption: Step 11: Use Download/Print to export the invoice as PDF
steps.description: Use Download/Print to export the invoice as PDF
steps.narration: Everything looks crisp and legal. Hit Download/Print, export the PDF, and send it off. Tutorial complete. Your coffee is still cooling. Go get that bread!

steps.caption: Step 12: Tutorial complete - create professional GST invoices in minutes
steps.description: Tutorial complete - create professional GST invoices in minutes
steps.narration: Flawless GST invoice in under two minutes. Now go get your money!
